subparts.text: Explain the concept of a forward contract and give an example of how it is used in financial markets. CO -1
Differentiate between hedging and speculation with examples CO – 3
What factors influence the pricing of interest rate futures? CO – 2
What is a short hedge using futures? Provide an example of when it would be used. CO – 3
What is the binomial option pricing model? Mention its basic steps. CO – 2
What does the term "underlying asset" mean in options trading? CO – 1
What does Value at Risk measure, and what are its key limitations? CO – 2
What is the primary objective of risk management in financial markets? CO - 1

questions.question_text: What are the different types of financial risks faced by firms? Explain how derivatives can be used to hedge credit risk, currency risk, and interest rate risk. CO - 3

Compare and contrast hedge funds and mutual funds with reference to their structure, investment strategies, regulatory environment, risk levels, and typical investor profiles. Along with the relevant examples. CO - 3
Discuss its application in portfolio and commodity risk management, highlighting the risks and benefits of using imperfect hedges. CO - 3
What is hedging? Explain how a short hedge and a long hedge using futures work, with suitable examples. CO - 3
Explain the concept of forward-spot parity. How does this relationship help in the pricing of options on underlying assets? Along with the examples. CO - 2
Explain the basic structure of an option contract. Compare and contrast call options and put options, including their payoffs, with appropriate examples. CO - 1
Identify and explain the different types of financial risks. Provide examples of how these risks can impact a company’s operations and financial performance. CO – 2
Describe the various hedging techniques available for managing financial risks. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of using futures, options, and forward contracts in hedging strategies. CO - 3
